records.ety: [OE. brayen, OF. breier, F. broyer to pound, grind, fr. OHG. brehhan to break. See Break.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Brayed (brād); p. pr. & vb. n. Braying.]

defs: To pound, beat, rub, or grind small or fine.
quotes: Though thou shouldest bray a fool in a mortar, . . . yet will not his foolishness depart from him.

records.ety: [OE brayen, F. braire to bray, OF. braire to cry, fr. LL. bragire to whinny; perh. fr. the Celtic and akin to E. break; or perh. of imitative origin.]

defs: To utter a loud, harsh cry, as an ass.
quotes: Laugh, and they Return it louder than an ass can bray.

defs: To make a harsh, grating, or discordant noise.
quotes: Heard ye the din of battle bray?

defs: To make or utter with a loud, discordant, or harsh and grating sound.
quotes: Arms on armor clashing, brayed Horrible discord.
And varying notes the war pipes brayed.

defs: The harsh cry of an ass; also, any harsh, grating, or discordant sound.
quotes: The bray and roar of multitudinous London.

records.ety: [OE. braye, brey, brew, eyebrow, brow of a hill, hill, bank, Scot. bra, brae, bray, fr. AS. brǣw eyebrow, influenced by the allied Icel. brā eyebrow, bank, also akin to AS. brū eyebrow. See Brow.]

defs: A bank; the slope of a hill; a hill. See Brae, which is now the usual spelling.
